text:
Deep learning developments in remote sensing change detection (CD) have been primarily driven by Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers. While CNNs are limited by a restricted receptive field that struggles to capture long-range spatial dependencies, Transformers suffer from quadratic computational complexity, making them resource-intensive for large-scale high-resolution datasets. In this paper, we explore the potential of the Mamba architecture—a state space model (SSM) featuring linear scaling and a selective scan mechanism—to overcome these limitations in CD tasks. 

We introduce ChangeMamba, a family of spatio-temporal state space frameworks tailored for three core CD subtasks: MambaBCD for binary change detection, MambaSCD for semantic change detection, and MambaBDA for building damage assessment. Each framework utilizes the cutting-edge Visual Mamba (VMamba) as a weight-sharing encoder to extract global spatial context from multi-temporal images. To effectively capture temporal dynamics, we propose three spatio-temporal relationship modeling mechanisms—sequential, cross, and parallel modeling—which enable the Mamba-based decoder to learn complex interactions between temporal features efficiently. 

Extensive experiments on five benchmark datasets (SYSU-CD, LEVIR-CD+, WHU-CD, SECOND, and xBD) demonstrate that ChangeMamba consistently outperforms state-of-the-art CNN- and Transformer-based methods in accuracy and efficiency. Furthermore, our architecture exhibits superior robustness against degraded input data, such as noise and blur. The results suggest that the Mamba architecture is a powerful and efficient alternative for remote sensing dense prediction tasks. Our source code is available at https://github.com/ChenHongruixuan/MambaCD.
